item.title: Integrating Enhanced Molecular Sampling, RNA-Specific Scoring Functions, and SILCS Technology for Small-Molecule Targeting of RNA
item.description: Increasing knowledge of the wide range of biological functions of ribonucleic acids (RNAs) have made them a critical target in drug development. Accordingly, modifying their function through targeting them with small, drug-like molecules for the ...

item.title: Learning from SILCS FragMaps: Combining Physics-Based Energies with Artificial Intelligence for Rapid Drug Permeability Prediction
item.description: Prediction of drug passive permeability represents an important component of rational drug design requiring continued development of methods to address this problem. Artificial intelligence (AI) methods have long been shown to be of utility for prediction ...

item.title: Synthesis, Redox Properties and Magneto-Optical Activity of a Homologous Set of Metallo-5,5-Dimethylisocorrole Complexes
item.description: A homologous set of 5,5-dimethyl-10,15-bis(pentafluorophenyl)-isocorrole (5-DMIC) complexes supporting NiII, PdII, PtII, or ZnII metal centers have been prepared and characterized. These complexes are readily accessible via modular synthetic routes and ...

item.title: Stereospecific Engagement of a Methyl-Sensitive ECL2 Anchor Site in 5-HT₂A by α-Substituted NBOH Derivatives
FluoFlow as a Family-anchored fragment flow matching for near-infrared fluorophore candidate discovery
item.description: Generative models could accelerate fluorophore discovery, but wavelength conditioning is difficult when red and near-infrared emitters occupy sparse, family-structured regions of chemical space. We introduce FluoFlow, a fragment-tree flow-matching ...

item.title: Utility of Acid Co-Product from Bipolar Membrane Electrodialysis as a Function of System-scale, Location, and Flexible Operation
item.description: Bipolar membrane electrodialysis (BPMED) produces acid and base from seawater, and is being primarily advanced for base generation, with co-produced acid needing valorization. Acid cost and utility depend on process scale, electricity prices, and ...

item.title: Parallel Evolution of Dirhodium Artificial Metalloenzymes Creates Distinct Reaction Environments for Selective Carbene Transfer
item.description: Artificial metalloenzymes (ArMs) combine the reactivity of transition metal catalysts with the selectivity and evolvability of protein scaffolds. Understanding different mechanisms by which scaffolds can impart selectivity would greatly facilitate ArM ...

item.title: Continuous Flow Electrophotochemical Minisci Reaction via Catalytic Benzophenone Regeneration
item.description: The use of benzophenone as a hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) photocatalyst is limited by catalyst deactivation through ketyl radical dimerization, often requiring stoichiometric loadings. Herein, we report a continuous flow electrophotochemical strategy that ...

item.title: On again, off again: thiol-Michael photoactivated dynamic covalent chemistry
item.description: Light-responsive dynamic covalent chemistry is a powerful tool that can provide spatiotemporal control of dynamic bond exchange when coupled with light as an external stimulus. Despite its promise, there is a need to develop new tools to harness light for ...

item.title: Extending Generative Machine Learning for Sustainable Polymer Design: An Integrated Workflow from Generative Models to Bio-Based Candidates
item.description: The discovery of sustainable bio-based polymers is a pressing need, as traditional synthetic polymers derived from fossil fuels pose significant environmental challenges. However, bio-based alternatives often occupy different regions of chemical space and ...

item.title: Impact of Chronic Hydration–Dehydration Cycling on Enamel Analog Microhardness and Crack Propagation: An in Vitro Model of Dry Environmental Stress
item.description: Objective: To evaluate the effects of repeated hydration–dehydration cycles on the mechanical integrity of enamel analog materials, focusing on microhardness reduction and crack propagation behavior under simulated dry conditions. Methods: Commercial ...

item.title: Fluorescent Metal–Organic Frameworks for Chemical Sensing: Design Strategies, Mechanisms, and Analytical Applications
item.description: Fluorescent metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have emerged as transformative platforms for chemical sensing, combining ultrahigh porosity, tunable optical properties, and versatile host-guest chemistry to achieve exceptional sensitivity and selectivity. ...

item.title: The Key Role of Solvation in Enhancing Electrochemical Promotion of Brønsted Acid Catalysis
item.description: Electrostatic polarization of catalyst-solution interfaces has been demonstrated to modulate the rate of liquid-phase heterogeneous Brønsted acid catalysis by up to five orders of magnitude. Previous studies in acetonitrile establish that maximal rate-...

item.title: All-Photonic Yellow-Red-Blue-Green Orthogonal Photoswitching for Rewritable Four-Color 2D and 3D Displays and Optical Memories
item.description: Light-induced color change is the defining and most fundamental characteristic of a photochromic material. Twofold switching between colorless and color or two different colors is known to humanity for centuries and has been used allegedly already in ...

item.title: Interpretable machine learning reveals physicochemical drivers of bio-crude yield in hydrothermal liquefaction of water hyacinth
item.description: Hydrothermal liquefaction (HTL) is a promising pathway for converting wet biomass into biocrude oil; however, the complex interactions among process parameters limit mechanistic understanding and optimization. In this study, an interpretable machine ...

item.title: Descriptor Adequacy as a Materials-Regime Principle in MOF Adsorption Machine Learning
item.description: Machine learning is changing how metal--organic frameworks (MOFs) are screened for gas storage and separation, but its reliability depends on a basic question: does the descriptor contain the physics that controls the adsorption property? Here we show ...

item.title: Excited Triplet States during Organofluorine Molecular Dynamics upon Electron-Impact—A Study of Electronic Structure and Molecular Fragmentation
item.description: The semiconductor industry’s reliance on high global warming potential (GWP) perfluorocarbon etchants motivates the search for environmentally sustainable alternatives. Although exchange scattering populates triplet states efficiently during electron-...

item.title: Guiding photochemical process intensification through kinetic diagnostics
item.description: Photochemical methodologies are commonly optimized towards yield, yet yield alone provides limited insight into productivity and scalability. Here, we demonstrate how simple kinetic diagnostics can guide photochemical process intensification by ...

item.title: In Silico Drug Discovery of Novel Fluorinated Phenylpropanoic Acid Derivatives as Tubulin Colchicine Binding Site Inhibitors: A Systematic Lead Optimization Study
item.description: Tubulin, a key structural protein involved in mitotic spindle formation, represents a validated anticancer target. The Colchicine Binding Site (CBS) on the beta-tubulin subunit offers a promising avenue for the development of novel antimitotic agents. In ...

item.title: Simple and reproducible ready-to-use TMB solution for enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay
item.description: The enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) is a cornerstone of modern laboratory diagnostics, yet its routine implementation is often constrained by the cost and logistics of chromogenic substrates. While commercial 3,3',5,5'-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) ...

item.title: Influence of the surface Rock-Salt Layer in Ni-rich NMC cathodes
item.description: Rock-Salt Layer (RSL) formation is one of the main degradation processes in Nirich cathodes for Li-ion batteries, giving rise to loss of capacity and cyclability. Our combined theory and experimental study examines in detail the impact of different ...

item.title: Varying Mechanisms Dominate Formate Formation in Electrochemical CO2 Reduction on Cu, Au and Pb: A Constant Potential DFT-based Microkinetic Analysis
item.description: Electrochemical CO2 reduction (eCO2R) offers a route to convert renewable electricity into fuels and chemicals while closing the anthropogenic carbon cycle. However, the technology remains immature and is limited by low selectivity and energy efficiency. ...

item.description: Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS) is a powerful technique to probe nano to microscale objects in solution. Among its main limitations is the requirement of steady-state conditions during measurement, restricting its applicability towards time evolving ...

item.title: Mobility Suppression Boundary Principle for Polymer Glass-Transition Temperature
item.description: Polymer glass-transition temperature (Tg) varies widely even among repeat units with similar visible chemistry and size. This manuscript formulates and tests the Mobility Suppression Boundary Principle (MSBP-Tg): within comparable chemistry-size fibers, ...

item.title: Tall oil pitch as a graphite feedstock: direct conversion of a biomassderived by-product into bulk graphite

item.title: Observation of ”negative” persistent photoconductivity effect at T > 200 oC in N-polar GaN/AlGaN/GaN heterostructures

item.description: Chlorinated impurities in industrial diisocyanates production significantly impact product quality and stability, yet their speciation remains an analytical challenge. This study demonstrates that 35Cl NQR spectroscopy, supported by DFT calculations, ...

item.description: Microfractionation is a prominent alternative for the discovery and characterization of bioactive natural products in complex extracts (e.g., plant, fungal, or microbial); however, the method faces limitations as it requires arduous scale-up for complete ...

item.title: Advancing polypharmacology-based drug discovery using deep generative models

item.description: Organic-inorganic hybrid halide perovskites have achieved power conversion efficiencies exceeding 26% in single-junction solar cells, yet phase stability remains the primary bottleneck for commercialization. The cubic α-phase of FAPbI3 spontaneously ...

item.title: Trogocytosis targeting chimeras (TrogoTACs) for targeted protein transfer
item.description: Cell surface proteins contribute to cellular identity and mediate a myriad of pathological and normal physiological processes. Multiple induced proximity strategies have emerged to manipulate the cell surface proteome, wherein a protein of interest can be ...

item.title: Genetically programmed biotemplating enables five-orders-of-magnitude conductivity enhancement in amorphous phosphate cathodes via 3D transport networks
item.description: We demonstrate that bio-directed amorphization enables intrinsically fast ion transport in phosphate cathodes. Using genetically programmed M13 bacteriophage, we synthesize amorphous FePO4nanowire networks in water at low temperature, with NaCl as the ...

item.title: Fluorine-Free Conjugated Polyelectrolyte Optical Nanosensor Array Enables Trace PFAS Detection and Discrimination
item.description: Environmental contamination of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) into water supplies has become a global concern due to their toxicity, incidental discharge, and long halflife. Despite advancements in portable sensors, the simultaneous detection ...

item.title: The Effect of Trialkyl Borates as Lewis Acids on Quinones for Electrochemical Carbon Dioxide Capture and Concentration
item.description: Electrochemical CO2 capture and concentration (eCCC) systems have the potential to operate at higher efficiencies than current thermal systems. Quinones have been the focus of many eCCC studies as redox carriers, as they can capture or release CO2 ...

item.title: Symmetric Atomic Orbitals Model and the Nature of Chemical Bonding
item.description: A conjecture of symmetric atomic orbitals model was proposed in 1984, through decades of studying all kind of molecular geometries, the experimental evidences of the conjecture have been found: there are causal relationship between molecular geometries of ...

item.title: Solid-electrolyte interphase reactivity with the bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide anion governs cycling instabilities of sodium metal anodes
item.description: Highly reversible and long-cycling Na metal anodes would enable Na-based batteries to approach energy densities competitive with established Li-ion technologies while leveraging earth-abundant materials. While several Na electrolytes have demonstrated ...

item.title: Nanomechanical Insights into Curing-Driven Morphological Evolution in Crumpled Polyamide Nanofiltration Membranes
item.description: Crumpled polyamide thin films are widely used as selective layers in nanofiltration membranes, where their nanoscale morphology is closely associated with water transport and solute separation. Although crumpled morphology is commonly discussed in terms ...

item.title: Mobile Proton Control for Improved Stability of Tyrosine O-Sulfation in Mass Spectrometry Analysis
item.description: Tyrosine O-sulfation is a posttranslational modification (PTM) in membrane and secreted proteins. This PTM plays important roles in several cellular processes, e.g., embryonal development, viral entry, and the immune system, including therapeutic antibody ...

item.title: Natural Orbital Correlation Analysis of Cluster Bonding: From Aromatic Clusters to Metallic Superatoms
item.description: We study electron correlation across 11 molecular and cluster systems using the number of effectively unpaired electrons ND (the Takatsuka–Head-Gordon index),computed from CCSD/def2-SVP natural orbital occupations (LANL2DZ for Au13 – ) over the complete ...

item.title: Platform for the synthesis and evaluation of verrucarol-based ribosome inhibitors
item.description: Trichothecenes are a structurally diverse family of fungal natural products whose potent cytotoxic, antifungal, and immunomodulatory effects make them both a threat to food security and a scaffold for therapeutic development. These sesquiterpenes share a ...

item.title: Synthesis and Comparison of the Properties of a Dihydrobenzo[1,3]azasilole NAMPT Inhibitor with its C-Analog
item.description: Rh2(II)-Catalyzed C–H bond amination of 2-silyl-substituted aryl azides construct dihydrobenzo[1,3]azasiloles via anN-aryl rhodium nitrene catalytic intermediate. Potential NAMPT inhibitors were synthesized from the azasilaheterocycle and its carbon-...

item.title: Determination and recovery of spin-states for spin-crossover complexes by means of X-ray wavefunction refinement
item.description: In this study, we present the first experimental determination of the spin-states of spin-crossover-active transition metal complexes by using X-ray wavefunction refinement. Two complexes–(NH4)2Fe(SO4)2 · 6H2O and Fe(phen)2(SCN)2–were investigated using ...

item.title: Sequence-Dependent DNA Translocation and Solvent-Engineered Dynamics in Graphene Nanopores
item.description: Graphene nanopores offer a promising platform for next-generation DNA sequencing because their atomic thickness can potentially enable single-nucleobase resolution. However, the translocation of heteromeric single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) through graphene ...

item.title: Algorithmically Constrained Dual-Paradigm De Novo Drug Design: Exploring Chemical Space via Parallel Ligand- and Structure-Based Framework Validated on BACE1
item.description: Isolated de novo drug design paradigms carry inherent vulnerabilities: ligand-based (LBDD) models risk unreliable extrapolation beyond their training domain, while structure-based (SBDD) genetic algorithms accumulate molecular size-bias across ...

item.title: Facet-Specific Catalytic Performance of Solution-Derived Epitaxial Bismuth Vanadate Photoanodes
item.description: Photoelectrochemical performance of BiVO4photoanodes is strongly governed by the interplay among anisotropic charge transport, surface electronic structure, and interfacial molecular interactions. However, these relationships remain difficult to ...

item.title: Sisyphus: A Topology-Compiled Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Platform with Structure-Only Input and Bayesian Parameter Refinement
Mask for Individual Sampling of Tidal and Ruminal Gas Contaminated Breath in Dairy Cows
item.description: Standardized breath sampling in dairy cows is essential for health monitoring and emission control. Yet, controlled measurements of and discrimination between tidal and ruminal gas contaminated breath are challenging. Here, we introduce a sampler designed ...

item.title: Evaluating Mechanical-Embedding ML/MM for Predicting Mutation Effects in Chorismate Mutase Catalysis
item.description: Predicting how mutations alter enzyme catalysis remains a central challenge in enzymology and enzyme engineering. Although QM/MM simulations can in principle compute the activation free energy associated with enzymatic reactions, their high computational ...

item.title: Automated Electrostatic Attraction/Repulsion Hydrophilic Interaction Liquid Chromatography Screening for Polar and Charged Analytes in Complex Matrices
item.description: Electrostatic repulsion hydrophilic interaction liquid chromatography (ERLIC) provides unique selectivity for highly polar and charged analytes, yet method development remains challenging. Careful optimization of organic solvent gradients and buffer ...

item.title: Product-reshaped gas-phase hydrodynamics govern CO2 electrolysis in gas diffusion electrodes
item.description: High-rate CO2electrolysis is commonly assessed using current density, single-pass conversion and Faradaic efficiency, but these global metrics do not show whether the local reaction environment required for selective multicarbon formation is preserved ...

item.title: Recent Advances in Sodium-Ion Diffusivity in Sodium-Ion Batteries: Mechanisms, Characterization Techniques, Materials Design, and Future Perspectives
item.description: One of the fundamental kinetic parameters governing the electrochemical performance of sodium-ion batteries is Ion Diffusivity. Though sodium-ion batteries have advantages when it comes to cost, sustainability, and material abundance in comparison to ...

item.title: Stability Trends of Surface and Subsurface Interstitial Transition Metal Species on CeO2(111)
item.description: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) on reducible oxides are generally assumed to consist of isolated metal atoms located at the surface and directly accessible to reactants. Recent studies on Pt/CeO2(111), however, revealed that Pt preferentially occupies ...

item.title: FIREX: Fast-Ion Replica EXchange enables rapid sampling of Mg2+ pairing with nucleic acids
item.description: We introduce Fast-Ion Replica EXchange (FIREX), a Hamiltonian Replica Exchangebased sampling strategy specifically designed to enhance the sampling of metastable divalent cation binding with phosphate groups, e.g. in nucleic acids. We parameterize the ...

item.title: Modelling Mixture Permeation across Microporous Membranes using the Maxwell-Stefan Diffusion Formulation
item.description: Binary mixture diffusion in microporous crystalline materials is characterized by a set of three M-S diffusivities: Ð1, Ð2, and Ð12. The Maxwell-Stefan (M-S) diffusivities Ð1, and Ð2 characterize interactions of guest molecules with pore walls. The ...

item.title: Multiscale Vehicle Traffic Air Pollutant Exposure and Equity Analysis in the United States using a Refined Emissions Exposure Surrogate
item.description: We estimate block-level emission intensities of nitrogen oxides (NOX) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) from on-road vehicle traffic for every census block in the United States, combining MOVES-based emission factors with street level traffic data. We ...

item.title: Regioselective distal C(4)–H arylation of naphthalene directed by simple acetamido group at position C(2)
item.description: Herein, we report preferential C(4)-arylation of the naphthalene in N-(naphthalen-2-yl)amides is with aryl halides in the presence of a palladium catalyst and silver salt under mild reaction conditions. The developed efficient protocol yields C(4)-...

item.title: Physics-guided modelling of temperature-dependent electrical conductivity of functional oxides
item.description: Functional oxide materials are widely employed across a range of electronic applications, where electrical conductivity and its temperature dependence serve as key screening metrics for material suitability. Across these applications, experimental ...

item.title: DATA-DRIVEN CAUSAL DISCOVERY IN CATALYSIS
item.description: Catalysts are essential for the transition towards more sustainable chemical processes. However, their design still relies largely on empirical strategies, limiting the systematic understanding of how synthesis variables and reaction conditions influence ...

item.title: PolyDiT: Masked Diffusion Transformers for Polymer Generation and Property Prediction
item.description: Polymer repeat unit representation is not neutral preprocessing for diffusion models because it defines the feasible set, constraint locality, and chemical granularity of denoising. We introduce PolyDiT, a bidirectional discrete Diffusion Transformer (DiT)...

item.title: Development of a High Throughput Automated Titration System Relevant for Extraction of Critical Metals
item.description: An accurate determination of the acidity (pKa) of extractants in biphasic solvent systems is crucial, as this physicochemical property impacts efficiency and selectivity in the recovery of technologically critical metals. The conventional manual or semi-...

item.title: Engineered Fluids Unlock Coupled Geological Hydrogen and Critical Mineral Recovery from Ultramafic Rocks

item.title: A Critical Evaluation of Domino and Cascade Reactions in Sustainable Organic Synthesis: Activation Modes and Green Metrics
item.description: While domino and cascade reactions are widely celebrated for their step economy and intrinsic bond-forming efficiency, evaluating their true sustainability requires moving beyond a purely molecular perspective to incorporate rigorous Green Chemistry ...

item.title: Memory-Aware SIMD-Enabled Permutationally Invariant Polynomial Code-Generation Procedure: Acceleration of the MB-pol Potential Energy Function as an Example
item.description: Permutationally invariant polynomials have been used to develop a variety of highly accurate potential energy surfaces. Particularly when combined with the many-body expansion, such potentials present a promising avenue for reducing the computational cost ...

item.title: Detailed Microkinetics for NOx-Containing Exhaust gas conversion through Automated Mechanism Generation within Correlated Uncertainty on Pt(111)
item.description: Three-way catalytic converters simultaneously oxidize hydrocarbons and reduce NOx species. Under stoichiometric conditions the oxidation of hydrocarbons requires the transfer of oxygen from NOx species. The manner in which this occurs and the intersection ...

item.title: Inverse theoretical spectroscopy via Monte Carlo sampling: From spectra to ensembles of molecular structures
item.description: A molecule’s structure determines its spectroscopic properties, and vice versa, molecular spectra are indispensable for elucidating its three-dimensional structure. Here, we present a Monte Carlo sampling scheme for generating an ensemble of molecular ...

item.title: Competing Chemisorption and Physisorption of CO2 in Halide-Functionalized Zirconium MOFs
item.description: By removing formate capping ligands from the zirconium nodes of NU-1000, a version of the metal-organic framework NU-1000 was generated having nodes terminated with hydroxo/aqua ligand pairs. Unexpectedly, this material can adsorb CO2 at low pressure ...

item.title: Zwitterionic Acyl Anion Equivalents from Carboxylic Acids via Acylsulfonylhydrazides: A Practical Organocatalytic Umpolung
item.description: The development of mild and practical acyl anion equivalents remains a cornerstone challenge in modern organic synthesis, as traditional umpolung strategies often rely on moisture-sensitive reagents, strong bases, or specialized N-heterocyclic carbene ...

item.title: Ferrielectric Quasiracemic Crystals Engineered from Enantiopolar Racemates
item.description: Ferrielectrics, materials with partly switchable polarization, enable functional responses such as pyroelectricity and piezoelectricity. However, purely organic examples and general design strategies remain scarce. Here we show that quasiracemates (QR), ...

item.title: Assessing cell survival and biosignature preservation of the extremely thermoacidophilic archaeon Acidianus manzaensis after exposure to Mars-like conditions
item.description: Considering the harsh surface conditions on Mars, terrestrial organisms that can survive and remain detectable after exposure to similar conditions provide invaluable models for guiding current and future search-for-life missions. To this end, we ...

item.title: Do Chemically Modified Oligonucleotide Therapeutics Require Environmental Risk Assessment?

item.description: The synthesis of tertiary fluoride-containing stereogenic centers remains a significant challenge. This is challenge is amplified when such units are embedded within vicinal stereocenters arrays, where both absolute and relative stereochemistry must be ...

item.title: Catalyst-controlled divergent strain-release amination and skeletal
High pressure synthesis for gold-backboned polymer
An Integrated CREATE Method for Teaching Polymer Synthesis
item.description: Teaching polymer chemistry in the undergraduate and graduate chemistry curricula is important to prepare students for the demands of the modern chemical workforce. Validated and inquiryguided teaching strategies for these topics, however, remain scarce. ...

item.title: Comparing Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory to Intermolecular Energy Decomposition Analysis Based on Absolutely Localized Molecular Orbitals
item.description: Energy decomposition analysis (EDA) can provide insight into the nature of noncovalent interactions, even though individual energy components are not uniquely defined by quantum mechanics. The present work examines the two titular EDA schemes and ...

item.title: Prospect to Boost Catalytic Activity of Precious Metals by Adsorption Atop Topological Insulators
item.description: We use first principles calculations and chemical bonding analysis to investigate how the adsorption on topological insulators modifies the chemical properties of thin films of catalytic metals, Pt, Au, and Ag. Topological surface states (TSS) present at ...

item.title: Machine learning approaches to predicting metal catalyst performance in olefin polymerization
item.description: Olefin polymerization is a cornerstone of the modern chemical industry, and the development of high-performance metal catalysts is essential for controlling polymer microstructure and properties. Traditionally, catalyst optimization has relied on resource-...
